behavioral_patterns.value:
Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email records from data breach
  • Step 2: Combine email addresses with passwords from previous breach databases
  • Step 3: Test credential pairs against enterprise portals, VPNs, and identity providers at scale using automation

Detection: Monitor for multiple failed login attempts from same source IPs against different accounts, especially when usernames match recent breach disclosures
behavioral_patterns.context: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

behavioral_patterns.value:
AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Process exposed email list through AI tooling
  • Step 2: Generate personalized phishing messages referencing the target organization
  • Step 3: Enhance targeting using job titles and LinkedIn data
  • Step 4: Deploy visually identical replicas of legitimate correspondence

Detection: Analyze email patterns for AI-generated content signatures and cross-reference sender domains against recent breach victim lists

Help desk social engineering bypass

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Gather valid email address and basic OSINT on target employee
  • Step 2: Impersonate employee in call to IT support
  • Step 3: Request password reset, MFA device reset, or account unlock
  • Step 4: Bypass technical authentication controls through human process exploitation

Detection: Flag help desk requests that coincide with recent breach disclosures and implement additional verification for reset requests

Real-time phishing relay (AiTM) attack

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Deploy reverse proxy between victim and legitimate service
  • Step 2: Forward victim credentials to real site in real-time
  • Step 3: Relay MFA challenge from real site to victim
  • Step 4: Forward victim's MFA response through proxy
  • Step 5: Capture authenticated session token

Detection: Monitor for authentication sessions with unusual network paths or timing anomalies between credential submission and MFA completion

MFA fatigue attack progression

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain valid user credentials
  • Step 2: Trigger repeated push notifications to user's device
  • Step 3: Continue bombardment until user approves out of frustration
  • Step 4: Gain authenticated access through user exhaustion

Detection: Alert on multiple consecutive MFA push notifications to same user within short time windows, especially outside business hours

Cloud-synced passkey compromise chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Target cloud account associated with synced passkeys
  • Step 2: Execute SIM swap attack against recovery phone number
  • Step 3: Exploit account recovery flow
  • Step 4: Gain access to synced passkey credentials

Detection: Monitor for SIM swap indicators followed by cloud account access from new devices or locations

Device-bound credential theft sequence

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Compromise endpoint device containing passkey
  • Step 2: Attempt extraction of device-bound authentication credentials
  • Step 3: Replay authentication without authorized user presence

Detection: Detect endpoint compromise indicators followed by unusual authentication patterns from the same device

Multi-workflow parallel attack execution

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Acquire large email record dataset from breach
  • Step 2: Simultaneously launch credential stuffing automation
  • Step 3: Deploy AI-generated phishing campaigns in parallel
  • Step 4: Execute help desk social engineering calls
  • Step 5: Maximize attack surface through coordinated multi-vector approach

Detection: Correlate multiple attack indicators (failed logins, phishing emails, help desk calls) targeting the same organization within compressed timeframes

snowflake_sql:
-- KTLYST Detection Rules
-- Generated from 8 rules
-- ============================================

-- Rule 1: Detect Behavior: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain


-- ID: IOC-BEHAVIOR-cb7b8dc0
-- Generation: template (success)
-- Description: Detects behavior indicator 'Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record' extracted from threat intelligence.
-- ------------------------------------------------------------
-- Behavioral Pattern Detection: Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record
-- Source: behavioral_0
-- TTP: T1078
-- Kill Chain: defense-evasion
-- Type: unknown

SELECT
    case_id,
    user_id,
    case_notes,
    case_description,
    created_at,
    'Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain

Behaviors:
  • Step 1: Obtain exposed email record' AS matched_pattern,
    'T1078' AS ttp_code,
    'behavioral_0' AS source_span
FROM case_notes
WHERE (
    REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_notes), LOWER('\\bEmail\ breach\ to\ credential\ stuffing\ attack\ chain\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Obtain\ exposed\ email\ record\\b'))
    OR REGEXP_LIKE(LOWER(case_description), LOWER('\\bEmail\ breach\ to\ credential\ stuffing\ attack\ chain\
\
Behaviors:\
\ \ •\ Step\ 1:\ Obtain\ exposed\ email\ record\\b'))
)
AND created_at >= DATEADD(day, -90, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP())
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

compiled_outputs.sentinel:
// KTLYST Detection Rules (KQL for Microsoft Sentinel)
// Generated: 8 rules from 8 indicators
// ============================================================

// Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign
// Category: initial_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1566
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| summarize EmailCount = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by SenderFromAddress, Subject
| where EmailCount >= 3
| sort by EmailCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Help desk social engineering bypass
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1621
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 4: Behavioral Detection - Real-time phishing relay (AiTM) attack
// Category: initial_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1566
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| summarize EmailCount = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by SenderFromAddress, Subject
| where EmailCount >= 3
| sort by EmailCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 5: Behavioral Detection - MFA fatigue attack progression
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 6: Behavioral Detection - Cloud-synced passkey compromise chain
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 7: Behavioral Detection - Device-bound credential theft sequence
// Category: credential_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1078
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize FailedCount = count(),
    UniqueIPs = dcount(IPAddress),
    UniqueLocations = dcount(Location),
    FirstAttempt = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastAttempt = max(TimeGenerated)
    by UserPrincipalName
| where FailedCount >= 5 or UniqueIPs >= 3
| sort by FailedCount desc
| take 100

// Rule 8: Behavioral Detection - Multi-workflow parallel attack execution
// Category: initial_access
// Source: Page 1
// MITRE: T1566
EmailEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(24h)
| where DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
| where AttachmentCount > 0 or UrlCount > 0
| summarize EmailCount = count(),
    UniqueRecipients = dcount(RecipientEmailAddress),
    FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated)
    by SenderFromAddress, Subject
| where EmailCount >= 3
| sort by EmailCount desc
| take 100
compiled_outputs.splunk:
``` KTLYST Detection Rules (SPL for Splunk)
``` Generated: 8 rules from 8 indicators

``` Rule 1: Behavioral Detection - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1078
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

``` Rule 2: Behavioral Detection - AI-assisted targeted phishing campaign
``` Category: initial_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1566
index=main sourcetype=mail action=delivered (attachment_count>0 OR url_count>0)
| stats count as email_count, dc(recipient) as unique_recipients, earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by sender, subject
| where email_count >= 3
| sort -email_count
| table sender, subject, email_count, unique_recipients, first_seen, last_seen

``` Rule 3: Behavioral Detection - Help desk social engineering bypass
``` Category: credential_access
``` Source: Page 1
``` MITRE: T1621
index=main sourcetype=authentication action=failure
| stats count as failed_count, dc(src_ip) as unique_ips, earliest(_time) as first_attempt, latest(_time) as last_attempt by user
| where failed_count >= 5 OR unique_ips >= 3
| sort -failed_count
| table user, failed_count, unique_ips, first_attempt, last_attempt

# KTLYST Detection Rules (SIGMA format)
# Generated: 8 rules from 8 indicators
# Convert to platform-native with: sigma convert -t <target>
---

title: 'KTLYST - Email breach to credential stuffing attack chain'
id: e77a17d4-bf8c-5405-99c5-01be701f4643
status: experimental
description: |
    Auto-generated detection rule.
    Source provenance: Page 1
level: medium
author: KTLYST
date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
    - attack.t1078
logsource:
    category: authentication
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        status: 'failure'
    filter_low_count:
        count|gte: 5
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity

date: 2026/03/24
references:
    - 'KTLYST source: Page 1'
tags:
    - attack.detection
    - attack.t1566
logsource:
    category: mail
    product: any
detection:
    selection:
        action: 'delivered'
    filter_attachments:
        attachment_count|gte: 1
    condition: selection and filter_attachments
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative activity
